summary: SchoolAutomaton should structure every AI-generated lesson around the highest-utility evidence-based techniques (retrieval practice and distributed/spaced practice are the two strongest per Dunlosky 2013 and Donoghue & Hattie's 2021 meta-analysis of 242 studies / 169k participants), woven into a fixed lesson flow: prior-knowledge activation -> pretest -> dual-coded explanation with worked examples -> faded scaffolding -> interleaved retrieval practice -> spaced review scheduling -> growth-mindset feedback. For spaced review, implement FSRS-6 via the maintained py-fsrs library (v6.3.1, MIT) rather than hand-rolling SM-2; it needs 20-30% fewer reviews for the same retention and degrades gracefully. Grade/age calibration should be driven by explicit, machine-checkable targets: a target Flesch-Kincaid grade level and Lexile band per grade band, plus Bloom's-taxonomy tiers that map directly to question difficulty. Every lesson and every question must carry rich metadata (Bloom tier, FSRS card state, concept/skill IDs, misconception tags, prerequisite graph edges, readability score) so the scheduler, the difficulty adapter, and the misconception-remediation logic can all key off structured fields rather than re-parsing prose. The LLM should be instructed to emit this metadata as structured JSON alongside generated content, and the readability target should be verified post-generation with a deterministic Python check (textstat) and regenerated if out of band.
concrete_specs: FSRS Scheduler config: Scheduler(desired_retention=0.9, learning_steps=(timedelta(minutes=1), timedelta(minutes=10)), relearning_steps=(timedelta(minutes=10),), maximum_interval=36500, enable_fuzzing=True). Per-profile desired_retention knob: 0.85 casual / 0.90 default / 0.95 pre-deadline.

FSRS Rating mapping: Again=1 (wrong), Hard=2 (correct with hint/slow), Good=3 (correct), Easy=4 (correct + instant). API: `card, review_log = scheduler.review_card(card, rating)`; persist via card.to_json()/Card.from_json(); read next due from card.due (UTC datetime).

FSRS forgetting curve: R(t,S) = (1 + FACTOR * t/S)^DECAY ; next_interval = S/FACTOR * (desired_retention^(1/DECAY) - 1). Reference DECAY=-0.5, FACTOR=19/81 for the fixed-decay variant; FSRS-6 uses optimizable decay (w20).
Per-profile FSRS daily caps: 20 new items + all overdue for default; lower new-cap (~10) for youngest band.

Readability targets by grade band (FKGL = Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level; Lexile = CCSS college/career-ready stretch bands): K -> FKGL<=1, BR40L-230L; G1 -> 1, 190-530L; G2 -> 2, 420-650L; G3 -> 3, 520-820L; G4 -> 4, 740-940L; G5 -> 5, 830-1010L; G6 -> 6, 925-1070L; G7 -> 7, 970-1120L; G8 -> 8, 1010-1185L; G9 -> 9, 1050-1260L; G10 -> 10, 1080-1335L; G11-12 -> 11-12, 1185-1385L; adult/unspecified -> FKGL 9-11, ~1100-1300L.

Sentence-length / vocabulary caps: K-G2 <=10 words/sentence and <=3 new terms/lesson; G3-G5 <=15 words/sentence, ~5 new terms, define inline; G6-G8 glossary + domain vocab; G9-12 full academic register. Regenerate if measured FKGL > target+1.5.
Readability verification lib: `textstat`; use textstat.flesch_kincaid_grade() per explanation section after generation.

Bloom's revised taxonomy difficulty tiers (1-6): 1 Remember, 2 Understand, 3 Apply, 4 Analyze, 5 Evaluate, 6 Create. Grade weighting: K-G5 emphasize tiers 1-3; G6-12 + adults include 4-6. Adaptive: +1 tier after 2 consecutive correct, -1 tier after a miss.
Lesson section enum (ordered): hook, objectives, prior_knowledge, pretest, explanation, worked_example, faded_example, practice, interleaved_review, elaboration, misconception_check, summary.

Item metadata fields: id, concept_id(s), skill_id, bloom_tier, difficulty, item_type(mcq|cloze|short|numeric|match), language, expected_answer, accepted_variants, distractors[].misconception_id, hint_ladder[], worked_solution_steps[], fsrs(state,stability,difficulty,due,reps,lapses,last_review), source_lesson_id, model, prompt_version.

Lesson metadata fields: id, topic, detected_language, grade_band, target_fkgl, measured_fkgl, lexile_band, objectives[](bloom_tagged), prereq_concept_ids[], concept_ids_taught[], misconception_ids[], estimated_duration, sections[], illustration_refs[], model, prompt_version, created_at.
Interleaving session mix: ~50-60% current concept, 20-30% related-concept due items, 10-20% prerequisite refreshers, shuffled so consecutive items differ in concept/type.

Feedback rules (inject into prompts): praise strategy/process not ability; on error use 'not yet' + next actionable step + optional hint; never praise effort alone without a strategy/correction; generate in detected language. Gamify review-consistency/streaks and items reaching FSRS Review state, not raw click points.
pitfalls: Do NOT hand-code FSRS weight math (initial difficulty, mean reversion, post-lapse stability, SInc with w15 hard-penalty/w16 easy-bonus) - the 21-weight formulas are error-prone and authoritative plain-text versions are hard to source; use the maintained py-fsrs library and treat its internals as a black box. SM-2 is a markedly weaker fallback (older, ~20-30% more reviews for equal retention).

FKGL is English-calibrated. For auto-detected non-English content the score is unreliable; fall back to language-appropriate proxies (sentence length + long-word share) and do not store a misleading FKGL. State this limitation in lesson metadata.

Growth-mindset feedback is frequently misimplemented: praising effort alone ('good try!') without a concrete strategy or correction is empty and can backfire. Always pair process feedback with the next actionable strategy. Ability praise ('you're so smart') is actively harmful to resilience.

LLMs do not reliably self-calibrate reading level or Bloom tier; never trust the model's self-reported readability/difficulty. Verify FKGL deterministically (textstat) and re-prompt on failure; sanity-check Bloom tiers against the verb used.

Spacing/interleaving feel harder and slower to learners and produce worse short-term performance (desirable difficulties); without UI framing students may rate the tool as 'less effective.' Surface long-term retention gains and streaks to counter this.
Over-interleaving across unrelated subjects (vs related concepts) harms novices and young learners; keep interleaving within a coherent concept neighborhood and reduce strength for the youngest band.

Daily review load can explode as the card pool grows; cap new items/day and prioritize overdue cards, otherwise learners abandon the queue. Lowering desired_retention reduces load but also long-term recall - expose it deliberately.

Don't let gamification reward raw activity (clicks/points) - that incentivizes guessing and gaming; tie rewards to mastery signals (FSRS Review state, spaced-review streaks) so incentives align with durable learning.

Pretests/low-stakes testing must be explicitly framed as ungraded; if learners perceive the pretest as a graded exam over un-taught material, it raises anxiety and undercuts the forward-testing benefit.

Misconception remediation only works if distractors are tagged with the specific misconception they probe; generic 'incorrect' feedback misses the conceptual-change opportunity. Require per-distractor misconception_id at generation time.

recommendations.title: Implement spaced repetition with FSRS-6 via the py-fsrs library, not a hand-coded SM-2
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add `fsrs>=6.3.1` to requirements. Create a thin `srs_service.py` wrapping Scheduler. SQLAlchemy: store card fields (state int, stability float, difficulty float, due datetime, last_review datetime, reps int, lapses int) plus `fsrs_card_json` Text for round-tripping via Card.to_json()/from_json(). Endpoint POST /review/{item_id} accepts a rating, updates the card, returns next due. A `due_items` query orders by due asc, limits to a daily cap (e.g. 20-40 new + all overdue) to avoid overwhelming the learner.

recommendations.title: Use a fixed, evidence-based lesson structure (the 'lesson skeleton') the LLM must fill
recommendations.how_to_apply: Define a Pydantic `Lesson` schema with a `sections: list[Section]` where Section has `kind: Enum(hook|objectives|prior_knowledge|pretest|explanation|worked_example|faded_example|practice|interleaved_review|elaboration|misconception_check|summary)`. Put the skeleton in the system prompt as a required output contract and pass it to Claude with `tools`/structured output so it returns JSON matching the schema. Frontend renders sections in order; gate later sections until earlier interactive ones are attempted.

recommendations.title: Drive grade/age calibration with explicit readability + vocabulary targets, verified deterministically after generation
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add `textstat` to requirements. After Claude returns lesson text, compute textstat.flesch_kincaid_grade() per explanation section; if it exceeds target+1.5 grades, re-prompt with the measured score and an instruction to simplify (shorter sentences, more common words, split long sentences). Store target_fkgl, measured_fkgl, lexile_band on the lesson. For non-English (auto-detected language), FKGL is English-tuned, so fall back to language-appropriate proxies (avg sentence length in words + share of long words) and instruct the model in the target language; note this limitation in the lesson metadata.

recommendations.title: Tier question difficulty by Bloom's taxonomy and tie tiers to both objectives and the SRS rating
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add `bloom_tier: int(1..6)` and `difficulty: enum` to the Question schema and `bloom_tier` to each Objective. Include a Bloom verb cheat-sheet in the prompt and require the model to tag each item. Store an item-level `p_correct` running estimate to refine difficulty. The adaptive stepper lives in the quiz service, not the LLM.

recommendations.title: Attach a rich, structured metadata envelope to every lesson and every item to power scheduling, remediation, and analytics
recommendations.how_to_apply: SQLAlchemy async models: Lesson, Section, Item, Concept, plus association tables for lesson<->concept (taught/prereq) and item<->concept. Store FSRS state on Item. Use a `misconceptions` table (id, concept_id, description, refutation_text). Require Claude to output all metadata in the structured JSON; validate with Pydantic before persisting; reject/regenerate on schema violation. Index Item.due and Item.concept_id for the scheduler and interleaver queries.

recommendations.title: Frame all feedback as process/strategy-focused growth-mindset messages, never ability praise
recommendations.how_to_apply: Store a `feedback_style` enum and inject explicit rules into the LLM system prompt for both content generation and the answer-grading endpoint: 'Praise strategy and process, not innate ability; on errors give the next actionable step plus an optional hint; use not-yet framing.' Provide 2-3 templated examples per outcome in the prompt. Generate feedback in the lesson's detected language. Gamification: award badges/streaks for review-consistency and for items reaching FSRS Review state, not merely for answering.

recommendations.title: Interleave related concepts in practice and reviews instead of blocking by single topic
recommendations.how_to_apply: The quiz/review service composes a session by sampling: ~50-60% current-concept items, ~20-30% related-concept due items, ~10-20% prerequisite refreshers, shuffled so consecutive items differ in type/concept. Use the concept association table to find related/prereq concepts. Expose an `interleave_strength` setting; reduce it for the youngest band to avoid overload.

summary: Ground SchoolAutomaton's gamification in Self-Determination Theory (autonomy/competence/relatedness) and flow theory, not in raw point-chasing. The strongest 2023-2025 evidence (Springer meta-analysis, ETR&D) shows gamification reliably lifts intrinsic motivation, autonomy, and relatedness but has MINIMAL direct impact on competence unless mechanics are explicitly tied to mastery and competence-supporting feedback — so XP/badges/streaks must be wrappers around genuine learning signals, never the reward itself. For a tool serving children of any age, the overjustification/crowding-out risk is real and stronger in younger kids: rewards must be framed as informational (showing growing competence) rather than controlling. Concretely: an XP curve that levels every node mastered, streaks with bounded forgiveness (auto-applied freezes, earn-back, rest days — never weekend amnesty alone), ~15 mastery-anchored badges, self-referenced progress visualization via a "knowledge tree", adaptive difficulty targeting ~80-85% success to keep learners in flow/ZPD, and deliberate avoidance of public leaderboards (which raise anxiety and demotivate the less-confident with no proven learning gain). The data model needs per-skill mastery tracking, an event-sourced XP/activity ledger, streak state with freeze inventory, badge progress, and daily-goal config — all tied to the anonymous resume-code profile.
concrete_specs: XP curve: xp_total(L) = round(100 * L**1.5); level_from_xp(t) = floor((t/100)**(1/1.5)). Constants BASE_XP=100, CURVE_EXP=1.5 (config-driven).
XP per answer: xp = round(BASE_ITEM_XP * difficulty_mult * (1 - mastery_before)) so re-grinding mastered nodes yields ~0 XP. Suggested BASE_ITEM_XP=10, difficulty_mult = item_difficulty(1..5)/3.
Adaptive difficulty target_success_rate = 0.83 (range 0.80-0.85); maintain rolling recent_accuracy per (profile, skill) over last ~10 items.
Streak freeze cap: 2 for free/standard users, up to 5 for long streaks (>=30 days); freezes earned through play only, auto-applied silently. Repair/earn-back window: 48h.
Rest days: configurable 0-2 planned non-breaking days per week (default 1 for under-13 age band, 0 default otherwise).

Mastery model: per-skill mastery in [0,1] updated by spaced-repetition (half-life regression or SM-2 ease). Node states: locked, available, learning, mastered (mastery>=0.85), needs_review (now>decay_due_at).
Grading LLM (Opus 4.8) structured output: {correct: bool, partial_credit: 0..1, concept_tags: [str], misconception: str|null, explanation: str, encouragement_focus: 'effort'|'strategy'|'progress'}.
Generation LLM input: {skill_id, mastery, recent_accuracy, target_success=0.83, known_misconceptions:[...], language, grade_level} to keep items in the ZPD.

~15 mastery-anchored badges (First Light, Root System, Deep Diver, Comeback, Spaced Master, Sharpshooter, Honest Explorer, Polyglot, Cross-Pollinator, Curiosity, Self-Beater, Tree Grower[tiered], Teacher, Resilient, Marathon of Mastery).

Combo/accuracy bonus: session-level multiplier for consecutive first-try-correct answers, cap the multiplier (e.g. max 2.0x at 8-combo) so it rewards focus without runaway grinding; combo never penalizes a wrong answer beyond resetting to 1.0x.
Daily goal presets (autonomy): Casual=5min/~50 XP, Regular=10min/~100 XP, Serious=15min/~150 XP, Intense=20min/~200 XP; user-selectable and changeable anytime.
Leaderboards OFF by default; if enabled, ability-banded leagues of ~15-30 similar learners, personal-best framing, opt-in flag on profile.
Optional challenge modes (opt-in): Timed Sprint, Boss Review (mixed spaced-review of due nodes), No-Hint Mastery Check, Explain-It (LLM-graded teach-back) — none gate core progression.

DB tables: Profile, SkillMastery, XpEvent (event-sourced ledger), StreakState, BadgeDefinition, ProfileBadge, DailyGoal, SkillNode/Prereq edges, ProfileSettings(age_band, competitive_opt_in, language).
pitfalls: Gamifying activity instead of learning: the meta-analysis shows competence gains are minimal unless rewards track real mastery — XP-for-time-on-task produces engagement metrics but not durable learning.

Overjustification/crowding-out, stronger in children: expected, controlling rewards for an already-interesting topic can REDUCE intrinsic motivation. Frame rewards as informational competence signals, vary them, and lean on effort/strategy praise.

Public leaderboards as a default: documented to raise anxiety in less-confident learners and demotivate, with no proven knowledge gain; they help only highly competitive users. Keep opt-in, banded, personal-best framed.

Loss-framed / FOMO streak pressure: 'you're about to lose your streak!' and limited-time countdowns create compulsive, anxious engagement (especially in younger users). Use gentle gain-framed reminders, bounded forgiveness, and rest days.
Unbounded forgiveness: stockpiling many freezes destroys the streak's meaning ('a safety net with no ceiling stops being a safety net'). Cap freezes (2, up to 5) and use a short earn-back window.

Ability praise and norm-referenced feedback ('you scored higher than 80% of students'): fosters fixed mindset and performance-avoidance. Use self-referenced ('better than your last attempt') and strategy/effort praise.

Punishing failure or wrong answers harshly (big XP loss, broken combo with penalty): discourages exploration and rewards safe guessing. Reward error-correction (Comeback badge) and metacognitive honesty (Honest Explorer).

Pay/grind-to-win currencies: never let money or low-value grinding buy badges, freezes, or progress; for an anonymous no-registration tool keep all gamification currency earnable through learning only.
One-size-fits-all across ages: a 7-year-old and an adult need different competitive intensity and forgiveness. Make mechanics age/grade-aware and admin-configurable, with kill-switches per mechanic.

Decay neglect: if mastered nodes never decay, 'durable learning' is unmeasured and spaced review feels pointless. Model decay (needs_review state) so retention is the thing celebrated (Spaced Master badge).

recommendations.title: Anchor every reward to demonstrated mastery, not activity — XP is a side-effect of learning
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add a SkillMastery table (one row per profile x skill). On each graded answer, update mastery via a spaced-repetition update (e.g. half-life regression / SM-2-style ease). XP_awarded = round(BASE_XP * difficulty_multiplier * mastery_delta_factor) where mastery_delta_factor decays toward 0 as mastery -> 1.0. In the LLM grading prompt, return structured JSON {correct: bool, partial_credit: 0-1, concept_tags: [...], misconception_detected: str|null} so the backend can attribute XP to specific skill nodes.

recommendations.title: XP & leveling curve: gentle early, sub-quadratic growth, with a concrete formula
recommendations.how_to_apply: Implement as pure functions in a leveling.py module: level_from_xp(total_xp) = floor((total_xp/100)**(1/1.5)); xp_to_next(total_xp). Store only total_xp on the profile and derive level on read (cheap, avoids drift). Surface 'XP to next level' and a progress ring in the UI. Make these constants (BASE=100, EXP=1.5) config-driven so they can be A/B tuned without migration.

recommendations.title: Streaks with bounded, mostly-invisible forgiveness — protect the habit, not the anxiety
recommendations.how_to_apply: StreakState table: current_streak_len, longest_streak, last_active_date (UTC date, computed in the user's tz), freeze_inventory (int), freezes_used_in_current_streak, rest_days_per_week, rest_days_used_this_week, is_perfect (bool). On daily login/session-complete, run a settle_streak() job: if gap==1 day -> increment; if gap>1 -> consume freezes/rest days to cover missed days before breaking; if break and within repair window -> offer earn-back. Never send guilt-tripping 'you're about to lose your streak!' push at-risk; phrase as gentle, optional reminders.

recommendations.title: ~15 mastery-anchored badges/achievements (not activity-counters)
recommendations.how_to_apply: Badge definitions live as data (BadgeDefinition table or seed JSON) with a machine-evaluable criterion (a small DSL or a tagged evaluator function key). A badge-evaluation step runs after each learning event against the event ledger + mastery table. Store unlocks in ProfileBadge (profile_id, badge_id, unlocked_at, tier, progress_numerator/denominator for tiered/in-progress display). Show progress toward locked badges ('3/5') to support autonomy and goal-setting.

recommendations.title: Progress visualization as a self-referenced 'Knowledge Tree/Map', not a ranking
recommendations.how_to_apply: Backend exposes GET /profile/{code}/tree returning nodes with {id, title, mastery, state, prereq_ids, last_reviewed, decay_due_at}. Frontend (Vue) renders an SVG/canvas force-or-DAG layout, color/animation by state. Compute decay server-side: a node drops from 'mastered' to 'needs review' when now > decay_due_at (derived from the spaced-repetition half-life). Offer an optional weekly personal progress recap ('you mastered 6 new nodes, your science branch is 60% green').

recommendations.title: Keep learners in flow via adaptive difficulty targeting ~80-85% success
recommendations.how_to_apply: Maintain a rolling recent_accuracy per (profile, skill). Feed target difficulty into the Opus 4.8 generation prompt: include the learner's mastery, recent_accuracy, target_success=0.83, and known misconceptions so it generates items at the right level and language. Adjust an item_difficulty field (1-5) on the fly. Log predicted vs actual correctness to recalibrate.

recommendations.title: Make feedback informational and competence-supporting, never merely evaluative
recommendations.how_to_apply: Standardize the grading LLM call to return {correct, partial_credit, misconception, explanation, encouragement_focus:'effort'|'strategy'|'progress'}. Build the encouragement string from self-referenced mastery deltas, not from XP totals. Vary phrasing/visuals so reward feedback is not perfectly predictable. Never show a bare 'WRONG / -10 XP'; always pair with the why and a path forward.

recommendations.title: Avoid the documented anti-patterns: public leaderboards, loss-framed streak pressure, pay/grind-to-win, and reward-for-its-own-sake
recommendations.how_to_apply: Architecturally: gate any leaderboard behind an explicit opt-in flag (default off), restrict to ability-banded cohorts, and never expose raw rank to non-opted-in users. Make freeze/badge currency earnable only through learning, never purchasable (the tool is anonymous/no-payment anyway — keep it that way). In notification copy, use gain/encouragement framing. Add an admin/config kill-switch per mechanic so harmful elements can be disabled per deployment or age band. Consider an age/grade-aware config: dial down competitive elements and dial up forgiveness for younger learners.

summary: Layered sanitize-classify-validate pipeline for an untrusted, minor-facing student prompt (OWASP LLM01:2025). See recommendations for the 5 layers and concrete_specs for exact schema, char-strip set, models, routing, audit fields, regexes, and module layout.
concrete_specs: Classifier: Claude Haiku 4.5 (classification only). Generator: Claude Opus 4.8. Never run the generator on raw prompt text.
Max prompt length 2000 chars/8KB UTF-8. Rate limit ~20/min, ~300/day per (profile_id, ip_hash) token bucket; HTTP 429 on breach.

Unicode strip set: unicodedata.normalize NFKC then remove U+200B U+200C U+200D U+2060 U+FEFF U+202A-U+202E U+2066-U+2069 and all C0/C1 controls except newline and tab; collapse whitespace; flag Latin+Cyrillic/Greek mixing.

Structured Outputs call: output_config.format type json_schema with INTENT_SCHEMA, temperature 0, small max_tokens, prompt caching on the static system prompt. output_config.format is GA (Nov 2025); output_format was beta.

StructuredIntent enums (all required, additionalProperties false): intent=study|test|refuse|crisis|clarify; subject=enum allowlist+other; topic=string max 120 chars; grade_band=early_primary|primary|lower_secondary|upper_secondary|adult|unknown; age=int 4-99 or null; language=ISO-639-1; constraints.difficulty=easy|medium|hard, constraints.length=short|standard|long, constraints.format_pref=visual|text|mixed; is_educational=bool; safety_flags=array of none|self_harm|violence|sexual|illegal|hate|pii|age_inappropriate; injection_detected=bool; off_task=bool; classifier_confidence=0..1.
Delimiter: per-request nonce secrets.token_hex(8) wrapping sanitized text in a student_input block tagged with the nonce id. Never fixed/guessable.

Routing: is_educational false OR off_task true to Refuse; self_harm to Crisis (resources, no LLM counseling); other safety_flags to Refuse+redirect; classifier_confidence under 0.5 to Clarify; injection_detected true to log+strike and proceed with clean fields only.
Crisis: crisis_resources.yaml by ISO country + global fallback; US 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, EU 112/local; non-dismissable front-end card; I-am-an-AI-not-a-professional disclosure.

Audit record fields: request_id, hashed_profile_id, ip_hash, ts, raw_length, language, removed_char_summary, suspicion_score, heuristic_hit_ids, classifier verdict (topic hashed), decision_type, reason, latency_ms, model+PROMPT_VERSION, token_usage. Default no raw text; RAW_CAPTURE_ON_FLAG env flag default false with N-day purge.

Heuristic regexes (raise suspicion_score, never block alone): ignore all previous instructions, disregard, you are now, system prompt, DAN, leading Assistant/User/System role markers, long base64 blobs, payload-splitting markers. Stored in patterns.yaml.

Module layout: backend/app/sanitization with preprocess.py classifier.py validate.py safety.py audit.py models.py prompts.py patterns.yaml crisis_resources.yaml; ratelimit.py middleware; tests/test_injection_corpus.py. Use grade_band/age + intent to avoid over-blocking legitimate older-student topics (WWII, reproduction, chemistry).
pitfalls: Relying on the system prompt or delimiters alone. arXiv 2604.23887 and Zylos 2026 show model-reliant defenses fall to adaptive attackers; only deterministic layers contain a breakout.

Interpolating raw/sanitized prompt into the generator reopens the same-channel vulnerability; the generator must receive ONLY validated fields. Skipping NFKC + invisible/bidi stripping lets homoglyph/zero-width attacks bypass regex and the classifier.

Letting the LLM counsel a self-harm case instead of routing to crisis resources (CA SB 243 / SAFE BOTs require crisis referral). Logging raw prompts by default (COPPA 2026); store salted hashes, raw only for flagged events.

Trusting structured-output conformance as the only validation (re-check enums, re-sanitize topic). Using Opus 4.8 for the cheap screen instead of Haiku 4.5. Anonymous resume codes make evasion trivial unless limits key on IP/device hash too.

recommendations.title: Raw prompt is untrusted data end-to-end; the generator never sees it
recommendations.how_to_apply: Frozen Pydantic StructuredIntent in models.py is the only thing passed to the generator; unit-test no raw_prompt parameter.

recommendations.title: Layer 0+1 length/rate limiting and deterministic pre-processing before any LLM call
recommendations.how_to_apply: sanitization/preprocess.py pure function (normalized text, removed_chars, suspicion_score, heuristic_hits); ratelimit.py SQLite counters keyed on (profile_id, ip_hash), HTTP 429.

recommendations.title: Layer 2+3 hardened CLASSIFIER on Haiku 4.5 then deterministic validation
recommendations.how_to_apply: sanitization/classifier.py nonce secrets.token_hex(8), temperature 0, INTENT_SCHEMA from Python Enums; sanitization/validate.py returns a Decision tagged union; Refuse/Crisis never reach generation; red-team tests/test_injection_corpus.py.

recommendations.title: Layer 4+5 child-safety moderation, crisis routing, generator, and audit logging
recommendations.how_to_apply: sanitization/safety.py with crisis_resources.yaml (ISO country + global fallback), non-dismissable Vue card; async audit_log + sanitization/audit.py record(event) fire-and-forget with an N-day purge.

summary: Use the async Anthropic Python SDK (AsyncAnthropic) with model claude-opus-4-8, thinking adaptive, and output_config effort per step. Full lessons use a multi-step pipeline (intent -> plan -> parallel per-section content -> visual specs); quizzes use one streamed structured call. Both get guaranteed JSON via output_config.format json_schema (client.messages.parse with Pydantic models), never response prefill (400 on 4.8). Make the big reusable pedagogy system prompt a frozen cache_control prefix so it hits the prompt cache across the many per-student calls; append volatile student data last. Stream long generations to dodge the SDK timeout guard. Detect language once and thread the BCP-47 code into every step.
concrete_specs: Model id exact, no date suffix: claude-opus-4-8
thinking={type:adaptive}; display defaults to omitted - leave it, never surface chain-of-thought to students
Effort in output_config: intent=low, plan=high, per-section=medium, quiz=high, visual-spec=low; max for hardest only
REMOVED on 4.8 (400): temperature, top_p, top_k, thinking enabled+budget_tokens, last-assistant-turn prefill
Structured: client.messages.parse(output_format=PydanticModel)->parsed_output; raw output_config={format:{type:json_schema,schema:{...,additionalProperties:false}}}
Schema SUPPORTED: type, enum, const, anyOf, allOf, $ref/$def, formats(date-time,date,email,uri,uuid), additionalProperties:false
Schema NOT supported (use prompt+Pydantic validator): minimum, maximum, multipleOf, minLength, maxLength, recursive, complex array constraints
Caching: cache_control={type:ephemeral} 5m=1.25x write, {type:ephemeral,ttl:1h}=2x write, read=0.1x; break-even 2/3 reads; max 4 breakpoints; 20-block lookback
Min cacheable prefix on Opus 4.8 = 4096 tokens; verify response.usage.cache_read_input_tokens>0

Pre-warm: messages.create(model=claude-opus-4-8, max_tokens=0, system=[{...,cache_control:{type:ephemeral}}], messages=[{role:user,content:warmup}]); incompatible with stream/thinking-enabled/output_config.format
max_tokens: lesson/section=64000, quiz=32000, intent/visual=2000-4000; STREAM >16000 via messages.stream()+get_final_message()
Opus 4.8: 1M input context, 128K max output (128K needs streaming); $5/1M input, $25/1M output
Retries: SDK auto-retries 429/408/409/5xx/529; client.with_options(max_retries=5); never retry 400/401/403/404
Typed exceptions: RateLimitError, BadRequestError, OverloadedError, InternalServerError, APITimeoutError, APIConnectionError; log response._request_id
Async: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(); high concurrency pip install anthropic[aiohttp] + AsyncAnthropic(http_client=DefaultAioHttpClient())
Batches (50% off bulk pre-gen): client.messages.batches.create(requests=[Request(custom_id, params=MessageCreateParamsNonStreaming(...))])
Multilingual: intent returns BCP-47 language; thread into volatile tail; JSON keys English, values localized; instruct idiomatic native phrasing
Resumed-session operator context: append {role:system,content:...} to messages under beta mid-conversation-system-2026-04-07 (do not edit cached top-level system)
Guardrail: client.messages.count_tokens(model=claude-opus-4-8, system=..., messages=...) before expensive fan-out
pitfalls: datetime.now/student id/resume code/unsorted json.dumps in the pedagogy prefix silently kills caching (cache_read stays 0); keep prefix byte-identical, inject volatile data after the breakpoint
Prefix under 4096 tokens on 4.8 will not cache (no error, cache_creation=0); pad/consolidate above 4096
Response prefill to force JSON shape returns 400 on 4.8; use output_config.format
Relying on schema minimum/maximum/minLength/enum-count for exactly-4-options or difficulty-1-5; silently stripped, enforce in prompt+Pydantic validator and regenerate
Sending temperature/top_p/top_k or budget_tokens (old Claude code) all 400 on 4.8; steer via prompting+effort
Non-streaming with max_tokens>16000 raises ValueError / risks ~10-min idle timeout; always stream and use get_final_message()
Firing N parallel identical-prefix calls at once: all miss cache until the first streams, all pay full write; fire plan first, await its first token, then fan out
Trusting parsed_output without stop_reason check: refusal may not match schema, max_tokens means truncated JSON
Not capping 4.8 verbosity in quiz explanations; use a positive length exemplar, not just be-concise
Disabling thinking to save tokens can leak reasoning into the visible answer (bad for students); keep adaptive thinking on
Sending correct_index/explanation to the Vue client at quiz delivery exposes them in network tab; keep server-side, reveal via grading endpoint
Structured outputs are incompatible with Citations (400) and with prefilling; do not combine
Synchronous anthropic.Anthropic() inside async FastAPI handlers blocks the event loop; use AsyncAnthropic
Defaulting effort to high everywhere wastes tokens on cheap steps; set effort per step

recommendations.title: Async SDK, model claude-opus-4-8, adaptive thinking, per-step effort
recommendations.how_to_apply: Centralize model id + per-step effort in config; thin LLM service injects thinking/output_config/max_tokens. Never take these from user input.

recommendations.title: Reliable JSON via output_config.format + Pydantic, not prefill
recommendations.how_to_apply: app/llm/schemas.py with validators; check stop_reason refusal/max_tokens before trusting parsed_output; reuse schema (24h compile cache).

recommendations.title: Frozen cached pedagogy prefix to cut cost across per-student calls
recommendations.how_to_apply: SYSTEM_PEDAGOGY constant per content-type with cache_control on last block; inject date/id/language in trailing user msg or role:system (beta mid-conversation-system-2026-04-07); max_tokens:0 pre-warm at startup.

recommendations.title: Lesson pipeline: plan -> parallel sections -> visual specs
recommendations.how_to_apply: Plain-Python async orchestrator (not Managed Agents). Persist plan+sections to SQLite as they land for resume-code recovery. Await first plan token before fanning out to seed cache.

recommendations.title: Quiz pipeline: single streamed structured call
recommendations.how_to_apply: Pydantic validators; regenerate (max 2 retries) on failure. Keep correct_index/explanation server-side; reveal via grading endpoint.

recommendations.title: Multilingual: detect once, thread language into every step
recommendations.how_to_apply: language:str in intent schema + run context; interpolate into volatile tail only (keep prefix byte-identical/cacheable across languages); per-language exemplars in language-keyed cached prefixes.

recommendations.title: Token/cost control, retries, error handling
recommendations.how_to_apply: try/except -> FastAPI responses; generation_usage table keyed by resume code; token guardrail in sanitize step; nightly Batches job for curated library.

recommendations.title: Tune Opus 4.8 behavioral defaults in the pedagogy prompt
recommendations.how_to_apply: Cached prefix carries a concise length/format spec + one positive output exemplar + an instruction to produce the complete artifact in one pass; keep thinking adaptive, do not set display:summarized.

summary: SchoolAutomaton should use a 3-tier visual pipeline. Tier 1 (default, free, instant): Claude Opus 4.8 generates SVG diagrams/charts/labeled figures and CSS/SVG-SMIL animations directly as text — zero per-image cost, infinitely re-styleable, crisp at any zoom, perfect for labels and multilingual text. Tier 2 (raster illustrations, only when a scene/character is needed): Replicate FLUX.2 family — default to FLUX.2 [dev] (black-forest-labs/flux-2-dev, ~$0.012/MP, ~2.5s) or the cheapest FLUX.2 [klein] 4B (black-forest-labs/flux-2-klein-4b, Apache-2.0, sub-second) for high-volume; FLUX.2 [pro] (black-forest-labs/flux-2-pro, $0.015 + $0.015/MP, ~6s) when typography/quality matters. For SVG icons/logos use recraft-ai/recraft-v3-svg ($0.08/vector image). Tier 3 (rare, opt-in animation): Replicate image-to-video (wan-video/wan-2.2-i2v-fast cheapest, ~$0.018-0.05/s) — but prefer Lottie/CSS for almost all motion. Everything is cached on disk keyed by a SHA-256 content hash of (model + normalized prompt + params). Verified Nov-2025 FLUX.2 launch pricing and IDs against the Replicate blog and model pages.
concrete_specs: Replicate raster default: black-forest-labs/flux-2-dev (~$0.012/megapixel, ~2.5s, 32B)
Replicate cheap/high-volume: black-forest-labs/flux-2-klein-4b (Apache-2.0 license, sub-second latency)
Replicate hero/quality: black-forest-labs/flux-2-pro ($0.015 base + $0.015/MP, ~6s)
Replicate max-typography (rare): black-forest-labs/flux-2-flex ($0.06/MP, ~22s)
Legacy budget fallback: black-forest-labs/flux-schnell ($0.003/image) and black-forest-labs/flux-dev ($0.030/image)
Vector icons/badges: recraft-ai/recraft-v3-svg ($0.08 per vector image, $0.04 per raster)
Image-to-video (opt-in only): wan-video/wan-2.2-i2v-fast (cheapest, ~$0.018-0.05/s) or wan-video/wan-2.5-i2v (480/720/1080p, up to 10s, optional audio); Kling kwaivgi/* at ~$0.07-0.14/s for premium

FLUX.2 default params: megapixels=1, output_format=webp, safety_tolerance=1 (strictest; 1=strict..5=permissive), num_outputs=1, store seed for reproducibility, supports up to 8 reference images for character consistency
FLUX.2 aspect_ratio enum: 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, 16:9, 9:16, 21:9, 9:21 (~1MP default, up to ~1.4MP)
flux-schnell extra params: go_fast=true (fp8 optimized), aspect_ratio adds 2:3/3:2/4:5/5:4, num_outputs max 4, output_quality 0-100, disable_safety_checker (API only, keep False)
Layout slots -> aspect/MP/tier: HERO 16:9@1MP/dev; INLINE_FIGURE 4:3 or 1:1@1MP/dev; QUIZ_THUMB & icon 1:1@0.25-0.5MP/klein; PORTRAIT_CHARACTER 3:4@1MP/pro; FULL_BLEED_MOBILE 9:16@1MP/dev
Cache key = SHA-256(canonical JSON {asset_type, model_id, model_version, normalized_prompt(NFC, lowercased, whitespace-collapsed), sorted params, seed, output_format})

Cache path: data/cache/visuals/<hash[0:2]>/<hash[2:4]>/<hash>.<ext>; SQLAlchemy visual_assets table (hash PK, type, model, params_json, prompt, lang, width, height, bytes, mime, created_at, last_accessed_at, hit_count)
Serve assets with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable; URL contains the content hash; Docker named volume for data/cache

Raster illustration prompt template: 'A friendly, colorful flat vector illustration of {subject}, simple clean shapes, soft rounded edges, bright cheerful palette, plain off-white background, no text, no labels, educational children's textbook style, age-appropriate for {grade}, safe for kids, clear and uncluttered, centered composition'
Safety/avoid clause appended to prompt: 'Do not include: scary imagery, violence, blood, weapons, realistic human faces, brand logos, watermarks, or any written text.'
SVG output contract from Claude: strict JSON {svg, alt, caption}; sanitize with lxml (reject <script>,<foreignObject>,external href, on* attrs), require viewBox, cap ~60KB
Animation: SVG SMIL/CSS @keyframes by default; Lottie via @lottiefiles/dotlottie-vue for rich motion; always wrap in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) + pause control
Labels are language-neutral: generate raster with 'no text', overlay translated labels as SVG <text> positioned by layout per detected_lang
pitfalls: Replicate output URLs expire (~1 hour) — you MUST download and persist bytes to disk immediately; never store or hotlink the Replicate-hosted URL.

Diffusion models (including FLUX.2) misspell text, especially non-English/non-Latin labels — never bake lesson labels into raster; use 'no text' prompts + SVG/HTML text overlays in the student's language.

FLUX.2 [dev] is open-WEIGHT but under a Non-Commercial license; running it via Replicate's hosted endpoint is fine (you pay per use), but do NOT self-host flux-2-dev/9B weights for a commercial product. For self-hosting only the Apache-2.0 flux-2-klein-4B is license-safe.
Cost scales with megapixels, not per-image, for FLUX.2 — a careless 4MP generation costs ~4x a 1MP one; pin megapixels=1 (or lower for thumbs) and use klein for high-volume small assets.

Replicate models have cold starts; the first call after idle can take many extra seconds. Generate asynchronously in a FastAPI background task, show a skeleton/placeholder, and rely on the cache for repeat hits rather than blocking the request.

Generating educational VIDEO with image-to-video models is expensive, slow, non-deterministic, and frequently physically/pedagogically wrong (hallucinated motion) — do not use it for core teaching; default to deterministic SVG/CSS/Lottie animation and gate Replicate video behind explicit opt-in + quota.

Unsanitized SVG is an XSS vector — Claude-generated SVG can contain <script>, event handlers, or <foreignObject>; always strip these server-side before storing/serving, and serve from a separate asset path with a restrictive CSP.

SQLite is single-writer; under concurrent generation, funnel cache-row inserts through one writer (or use INSERT OR IGNORE on the hash PK) and write files atomically (temp + os.replace) to avoid partial/corrupt cached assets.
Don't let the LLM choose arbitrary image dimensions — it breaks responsive layouts and inflates cost; constrain to the fixed LayoutSlot enum and reserve space in Vue to avoid layout shift.

Model IDs and prices verified Nov-2025/June-2026; Replicate/BFL iterate fast (FLUX.2 launched Nov 2025). Keep model_id and model_version in config and in the cache key so a model upgrade invalidates the cache cleanly instead of serving mismatched assets.

Keep safety_tolerance at the strictest setting and keep the Replicate safety checker ENABLED (disable_safety_checker=False) for a children's product; layer your own Claude-based prompt moderation before generation.

recommendations.title: Make Claude-generated SVG the DEFAULT visual, not raster
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add a `generate_svg` tool/path in the LLM service: a dedicated Claude call (or a section of the lesson-generation call) that returns strict JSON `{"svg": "<svg ...>", "alt": "...", "caption": "..."}`. Validate server-side: parse with lxml, reject <script>/<foreignObject>/external href/on* attributes (XSS), enforce a viewBox, cap byte size (~60KB). Store the sanitized SVG in the same cache table as raster. Set width=100% height=auto in the Vue component and use `currentColor`/CSS vars so it themes automatically. Use a decision flag from intent extraction: visual_kind in {diagram, chart, illustration, photo} -> SVG for diagram/chart, Replicate for illustration/photo.

recommendations.title: Use FLUX.2 [dev] as the raster default, klein-4B for high volume, pro for hero images
recommendations.how_to_apply: Config-drive the model per use: `RASTER_MODEL_DEFAULT=black-forest-labs/flux-2-dev`, `RASTER_MODEL_CHEAP=black-forest-labs/flux-2-klein-4b`, `RASTER_MODEL_HERO=black-forest-labs/flux-2-pro`. Call via `replicate` async client (httpx-based) inside a FastAPI background task; persist the returned image bytes to disk, never hotlink Replicate URLs (they expire ~1h). Default params: `megapixels=1`, `output_format=webp`, `aspect_ratio` from layout, `safety_tolerance=1` (strictest), `seed` stored for reproducibility. Budget: at $0.012/MP a 1MP image is ~1.2 cents; with caching, repeat lessons cost $0.

recommendations.title: Generate SVG/CSS animation by default; reserve Replicate video for rare cinematic moments
recommendations.how_to_apply: Default `animation_kind` from intent: `css_svg` for anything diagrammatic; only set `replicate_video` when the student explicitly taps 'bring this to life' on a Tier-2 illustration. Gate video behind a per-profile daily quota (it is the only meaningful cost). Cache the resulting MP4 by content hash exactly like images. Always wrap motion in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` and provide a pause control.

recommendations.title: Cache every asset on disk by a deterministic content hash
recommendations.how_to_apply: Before any Replicate/SVG generation, compute the hash and SELECT the row; on hit, return the existing path. On miss, generate, write atomically (temp file + os.replace), insert the row, return. Run a periodic LRU eviction (delete files where last_accessed_at older than N days AND hit_count low) to bound disk. In Docker Compose, mount data/cache as a named volume so the cache survives redeploys. Include the hash in the asset URL so the immutable Cache-Control header is safe.

recommendations.title: Standardize kid-safe, consistent, labelled illustration prompts via a server-side template
recommendations.how_to_apply: Template: `"A friendly, colorful flat vector illustration of {subject}, simple clean shapes, soft rounded edges, bright cheerful palette, plain off-white background, no text, no labels, educational children's textbook style, age-appropriate for {grade}, safe for kids, clear and uncluttered, centered composition"`. Global negative/avoid (put in prompt since FLUX uses positive prompting): append `"Do not include: scary imagery, violence, blood, weapons, realistic human faces, brand logos, watermarks, or any written text."` Keep `safety_tolerance=1`. Maintain per-lesson style consistency by fixing the same `seed` family and reusing a reference image (FLUX.2 accepts up to 8) of an established character/mascot. Render labels as a separate SVG <text> overlay positioned by the lesson layout, translated per `detected_lang`.

recommendations.title: Pick aspect ratios and sizes to match fixed lesson layout slots
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add a `LayoutSlot` enum in Python with `(aspect_ratio, megapixels, model_tier)` per slot; the lesson generator tags each requested visual with a slot, and the visual service derives Replicate params from it. In Vue/Tailwind use aspect-[16/9] / aspect-square utility classes matching the slot so the skeleton reserves correct space before the asset loads. Generate WEBP for raster (smaller) with a PNG fallback only where transparency over arbitrary backgrounds is needed.

recommendations.title: Use recraft-v3-svg for crisp vector ICONS/badges that Claude shouldn't hand-author
recommendations.how_to_apply: Use it sparingly and cache aggressively (icons are reused across the whole app, so cost amortizes to ~$0). Generate the badge/icon set once at design time or lazily on first unlock, store the SVG in the visual_assets cache, and recolor via CSS to fit themes. Prefer Claude-SVG for anything that must encode specific lesson data (a labeled diagram); use Recraft for stylized decorative vectors.

summary: SchoolAutomaton's frontend should be a thin, playful Vue 3.5 + Vite 7 + TS + Tailwind v4 SPA organized around three flows (prompt -> generation/loading -> lesson reader OR interactive test -> results) with Pinia for state and Vue Router for routes. The design system is "soft, rounded, high-contrast playful but accessible": a token-driven Tailwind v4 @theme palette, a rounded friendly variable font (Lexend as default + Atkinson Hyperlegible & OpenDyslexic as user-selectable accessibility options), and motion via @vueuse/motion for declarative micro-interactions plus GSAP (now fully free incl. all plugins since v3.13) for celebratory result/quiz sequences, with Lottie (@lottiefiles/dotlottie-vue) reserved for reward animations. Interactive question components (MCQ, true/false, fill-blank, drag-drop matching/ordering, image hotspot) are built as a discriminated-union-typed component family with delightful, accessible feedback, all keyboard-operable and respecting prefers-reduced-motion. Untrusted LLM/Replicate output is hard-sanitized: SVG via DOMPurify in SVG profile (or rendered through ), markdown via markdown-it + DOMPurify, and math via KaTeX — never raw v-html. Accessibility is first-class (WCAG 2.2 AA, large touch targets for young kids, dyslexia mode, reduced-motion, full keyboard/SR support), and the whole app is mobile-first responsive.
concrete_specs: package.json deps: vue@^3.5.31, vue-router@^4.5, pinia@^3, pinia-plugin-persistedstate@^4, @vueuse/core@^11, @vueuse/motion@^2, gsap@^3.13, @lottiefiles/dotlottie-vue@latest, markdown-it@^14, markdown-it-katex (or @vscode/markdown-it-katex), katex@^0.16, dompurify@^3, vuedraggable@^4 (vue.draggable.next), vue-i18n@^10/^11, @fontsource/lexend, @fontsource/atkinson-hyperlegible

package.json devDeps: vite@^7, @vitejs/plugin-vue@^5, @tailwindcss/vite@^4, tailwindcss@^4, typescript@~5.6, vue-tsc@^2, vitest@^2, @vue/test-utils@^2, @playwright/test, @axe-core/playwright, eslint@^9, prettier
vite.config.ts plugins: [vue(), tailwindcss()] (no PostCSS, no tailwind.config.js)

src/assets/main.css: `@import "tailwindcss";` then `@theme { --color-primary: ...; --color-success: ...; --color-error: ...; --font-sans: 'Lexend', system-ui, sans-serif; --radius-card: 1.25rem; --text-base-size: 18px; }`

Folder structure: src/ { main.ts, App.vue, router/index.ts, stores/{session,prompt,generation,lesson,test,prefs}.ts, views/{HomeView,LoadingView,LessonView,TestView,ResultsView,ResumeView,admin/*}.vue, components/{common,layout,gamification}/*.vue, components/questions/{QuestionRenderer,McqQuestion,TrueFalseQuestion,FillBlankQuestion,MatchQuestion,OrderQuestion,HotspotQuestion,AnswerFeedback}.vue, components/content/{SafeContent,LessonSection,MathBlock}.vue, composables/{useReducedMotion,useCelebration,useGeneration,useApi}.ts, lib/{safeContent.ts,api.ts}, types/{question.ts,session.ts,api.ts}, locales/*.json, assets/ }

Question discriminated union: type Question = McqQuestion | TrueFalseQuestion | FillBlankQuestion | MatchQuestion | OrderQuestion | HotspotQuestion; each has { id:string; kind:'mcq'|'truefalse'|'fillblank'|'match'|'order'|'hotspot'; prompt:string; explanation?:string; ...kind-specific fields }
AnswerEvent normalized payload: { questionId: string; value: unknown; isCorrect: boolean; latencyMs: number }
Routes: '/', '/loading/:sessionId', '/lesson/:sessionId', '/test/:sessionId', '/results/:sessionId', '/resume', '/admin' (lazy + auth guard)
DOMPurify config for SVG: DOMPurify.sanitize(svg, { USE_PROFILES: { svg: true, svgFilters: true }, FORBID_TAGS: ['script','foreignObject'], FORBID_ATTR: [/^on/i] })
markdown-it config: markdownit({ html: false, linkify: true, typographer: true }).use(katexPlugin) then DOMPurify.sanitize before v-html
Accessibility prefs (usePrefs, persisted): { theme: 'default'|'highcontrast', font: 'lexend'|'atkinson'|'opendyslexic', fontScale: 1|1.15|1.3, reducedMotion: boolean, sound: boolean, locale: string }
WCAG targets: contrast >=4.5:1 (text), touch target >=44px (48px young-learner density), prefers-reduced-motion honored, aria-live='polite' for feedback/score, no color-only meaning
Theme switching via document.documentElement.dataset.theme and [data-theme] CSS var overrides; font via --font-sans override
pitfalls: Do NOT keep a tailwind.config.js with Tailwind v4 — config is now CSS-first via @theme and the @tailwindcss/vite plugin; mixing the old PostCSS/JS-config path with v4 causes broken or duplicated tokens.

Never bind LLM/Replicate output to v-html, :href, or :src without sanitization — v-html bypasses Vue escaping and :href/:src do not block javascript: URLs; this is the single biggest XSS risk in this app.

vuedraggable/Sortable.js drag-drop is NOT keyboard-accessible by default and is hard for young children to perform on touch; ship a select-then-place keyboard/click fallback for every match/order/hotspot question or it fails WCAG 2.1.1.
Do not force a dyslexia font on everyone — research is mixed on OpenDyslexic; offer Lexend/Atkinson/OpenDyslexic as a user-selectable option (default Lexend) per BDA guidance.
Reserve GSAP and Lottie for the results/reward route and lazy-load them; loading heavy animation libs on the prompt/home screen hurts first-load on the low-end devices children often use.

Gate ALL motion (GSAP, @vueuse/motion, Lottie) behind prefers-reduced-motion AND the in-app toggle in one central composable; scattered animation calls will inevitably miss the reduced-motion check and harm vestibular/attention-sensitive users.
Keep DOMPurify and KaTeX patched in CI — past DOMPurify SVG namespace-confusion mutation-XSS bypasses (<2.2.2) show stale versions reintroduce holes; pin to ^ and run dependency audit.
Don't render hotspot regions as bare clickable SVG <path> elements — they are not reliably focusable/labelable; overlay real <button> elements positioned over the image for SR/keyboard support.
Do not encode correct/incorrect using color only — colorblind and young pre-literate users need icon + text + (optional) sound as well.

The resume code is a convenience cache only; never trust the client-stored code/profile for authorization — the FastAPI server must remain the source of truth, and admin auth must be entirely separate from the learner resume code.

recommendations.title: Adopt the exact 2025-2026 core stack with pinned versions
recommendations.how_to_apply: package.json deps: vue@^3.5, vue-router@^4.5, pinia@^3, @tailwindcss/vite@^4, tailwindcss@^4; devDeps: vite@^7, @vitejs/plugin-vue@^5, typescript@~5.6, vue-tsc@^2, vitest@^2, @vue/test-utils@^2, @playwright/test, eslint@^9, prettier. In vite.config.ts: plugins: [vue(), tailwindcss()]. In src/assets/main.css use a single `@import "tailwindcss";` then a `@theme { ... }` block. Delete any tailwind.config.js.

recommendations.title: Structure routes + Pinia stores around the three learner flows
recommendations.how_to_apply: Define stores with defineStore('session', () => { ... return {...} }) setup syntax. The loading route polls or opens an EventSource to the FastAPI generation endpoint, writing into useGenerationStore.status ('queued'|'generating'|'ready'|'error') and partial; on 'ready' it router.push to '/lesson' or '/test' based on usePromptStore.mode. Guard /admin with router.beforeEach checking an admin token (separate from learner resume code). Store resume code only client-side as a convenience; server is source of truth.

recommendations.title: Build a token-driven, playful-but-accessible design system in Tailwind v4 @theme
recommendations.how_to_apply: In main.css @theme define semantic tokens: --color-bg, --color-surface, --color-primary, --color-on-primary, --color-success, --color-error, --color-accent (each meeting 4.5:1 against its on-color), --font-sans: 'Lexend', system-ui, sans-serif, --radius-card, --shadow-soft. Add [data-theme="highcontrast"] and [data-theme="dyslexia"] override blocks. usePrefsStore sets document.documentElement.dataset.theme and a --font-sans override (Atkinson/OpenDyslexic). Self-host fonts via @fontsource/lexend, @fontsource/atkinson-hyperlegible, and OpenDyslexic woff2 to avoid CDN/privacy issues for minors. Never encode meaning in color alone — pair with icon/text (correct/incorrect uses check/cross + label).

recommendations.title: Use a layered motion strategy: @vueuse/motion for micro-interactions, GSAP for sequences, Lottie for rewards
recommendations.how_to_apply: Install @vueuse/motion, gsap, @lottiefiles/dotlottie-vue. Register MotionPlugin in main.ts. Create a useReducedMotion() composable that reads window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') OR usePrefs.reducedMotion and, when true, sets GSAP global gsap.globalTimeline.timeScale or simply swaps animated transitions for instant state changes, and passes :speed/:autoplay=false or a static frame to Lottie. Wrap celebration logic in a single useCelebration() composable so the reduced-motion check lives in one place. Keep .dotlottie reward files tiny and lazy-load gsap only on the results route via dynamic import.

recommendations.title: Implement interactive question components as a typed, polymorphic family with shared accessible feedback
recommendations.how_to_apply: Create src/components/questions/ with one file per kind plus QuestionRenderer.vue and AnswerFeedback.vue, and types in src/types/question.ts. Drive scoring in useTestStore.submitAnswer(answerEvent). For match/order, render a parallel keyboard mode: each draggable also gets role/tabindex, Enter to 'pick up', arrow/Enter to 'drop', with aria-live announcing moves; vuedraggable handles the pointer/touch path. Hotspot: overlay absolutely-positioned <button> regions (not bare SVG paths) over the image so they are focusable/labeled; show coordinates only as fallback. AnswerFeedback uses role='status' aria-live='polite', shows a check/cross icon + localized text + GSAP/@vueuse micro-bounce (reduced-motion aware), and never relies on color alone.

recommendations.title: Hard-sanitize all LLM/Replicate output: SVG, markdown, and math through a single trusted boundary
recommendations.how_to_apply: Create src/lib/safeContent.ts exporting renderMarkdown(md:string):string (markdown-it{html:false,linkify:true} + markdown-it-katex, then DOMPurify.sanitize(html,{ADD_TAGS:['math',...],USE_PROFILES:{html:true,svg:true,mathMl:true}})) and renderSvg(svg:string):string (DOMPurify SVG profile + FORBID_TAGS:['script','foreignObject'], FORBID_ATTR:[/^on/]). A <SafeContent :markdown> wrapper component calls these and binds via v-html only after sanitize. Add a strict CSP header from FastAPI (no inline script, img-src restricted to your domain + Replicate CDN) as defense-in-depth. Keep dompurify and katex on the latest patch in CI dependency checks.

recommendations.title: Make accessibility, i18n, and responsiveness structural, not bolted-on
recommendations.how_to_apply: Add vue-i18n (lazy import of /src/locales/{lng}.json; set locale from session.locale returned by the API). Build an AccessibilityPanel.vue (gear/cog in header) writing usePrefs, persisted to localStorage and synced to server profile. Add @axe-core/playwright a11y assertions to e2e covering Home, LessonReader, each question type, and Results. Use Tailwind responsive prefixes mobile-first (base = phone, sm:/md:/lg: enhance). Ensure every interactive element is a real <button>/<a>/<input> or has correct role+tabindex+keydown handlers. Announce score/streak changes via a single aria-live region in the Results view.
